reasoning: Powell's term ends in May 2026. Applying the traditional 3-4 month lead time for Fed Chair nominations, the expected announcement would ordinarily occur in January or February 2026. This base rate firmly supports Trump waiting until 2026 (resolving YES to refraining in 2025). However, Trump frequently circumvents traditional timelines and has an adversarial history with Powell. Furthermore, his economic advisors notably floated the 'shadow chair' concept in late 2024 to potentially undermine Powell early. Although advisors later downplayed formalizing a shadow chair due to bond market risks, Trump's tendency to dictate narratives spontaneously via social media makes a late-2025 announcement highly likely. Balancing the structural timeline against Trump's behavioral unpredictability, I assign a 38% probability that he refrains from making an announcement in 2025.
